definitions: You cannot enjoy the benefits of two mutually exclusive things.
You cannot simultaneously possess something and consume it.
You must choose between two desirable but incompatible options.
examples: She wants a high-paying job but doesn't want to work long hours; you can't have your cake and eat it too.
He wants to save money but also buy expensive gadgets; you can't have your cake and eat it too.
You want to live in the city and have a big yard? You can't have your cake and eat it too.
lemma: you can't have your cake and eat it too
synonyms: you can't have it both ways
you can't burn the candle at both ends
source: you can't have your cake and eat it too
